title: Kconfig:68 LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY range 150-960 MHz too permissive
description:
Completeness check: Round 1-2 missed Kconfig validation gaps.

WHAT: LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY has range 150000000-960000000 Hz:
```
config LICHEN_LORA_FREQUENCY
    int "LoRa frequency in Hz"
    default 915000000
    range 150000000 960000000
```

WHY BUG: This range is far too permissive:
1. LoRa chips have specific frequency ranges (e.g., SX1276: 137-175 MHz, 410-525 MHz, 862-1020 MHz)
2. ISM bands have regulatory limits (433 MHz, 868 MHz, 915 MHz are main bands)
3. A user could configure 200 MHz which most LoRa chips can't transmit on
4. Even within chip range, frequencies between bands (e.g., 600 MHz) are illegal to transmit

IMPACT: User misconfiguration could:
1. Silently fail to transmit (chip rejects frequency)
2. Transmit on illegal frequency (regulatory violation)
3. Damage hardware if frequency is outside safe range

FIX:
1. Add runtime validation in lichen_lora_l2_start() against known bands
2. Or: Split into band selection + channel offset for safer configuration
3. Or: Add BUILD_ASSERT or runtime warning for frequencies outside known ISM bands
4. At minimum: Document that the user is responsible for regulatory compliance
